2605.03450.title: Retrieving Floods without Floodlights: Topic Models as Binary Classifiers for Extreme Climate Events in German News
2605.03450.paper_summary: In studies of media coverage of extreme climate events, NLP methods have become indispensable for identifying relevant texts in large news databases. Still, enough annotated data to train accurate deep learning-based classifiers from scratch is often not available. Topic Models have the advantage of being both unsupervised and interpretable, but are typically used only for exploratory analysis or data characterisation. In this study, we investigate how to employ Topic Models as binary classifiers for refining the retrieval of relevant news about seven types of extreme climate events in the German media. Our method relies on the posterior distributions estimated by Topic Models to select relevant documents, without modifying their training procedure. Using an annotated sample to guide the evaluation, we show that the probabilities assigned to keywords used to query news databases can also be informative for selecting relevant topics and improve sample precision. We compare our results to a fine-tuned text embedding classifier and an open-weight LLM, discussing observed trade-offs, e.g. the LLM's lowest precision. Moreover, we show that results are hazard-dependent, which speaks against considering climate events as a single category in NLP tasks.
2605.03450.paper_summary_zh: 在對極端氣候事件的媒體報導進行研究時，NLP 方法已成為在大型新聞數據庫中識別相關文本的不可或缺工具。儘管如此，通常沒有足夠的標註數據來從頭訓練準確的基於深度學習的分類器。主題模型的優勢在於它們既是無監督的又是可解釋的，但通常僅用於探索性分析或數據特徵化。在本研究中，我們探討如何將主題模型用作二元分類器，以精煉德國媒體中有關七種類型的極端氣候事件的相關新聞檢索。我們的方法依賴於主題模型估計的後驗分佈來選擇相關文檔，而不修改其訓練過程。通過使用標註樣本來指導評估，我們顯示用於查詢新聞數據庫的關鍵詞所分配的概率也可以對選擇相關主題提供信息，並提高樣本精度。我們將結果與微調的文本嵌入分類器和開放權重的 LLM 進行比較，討論觀察到的權衡，例如 LLM 的最低精度。此外，我們顯示結果是依賴於危害的，這反對在 NLP 任務中將氣候事件視為單一類別。
2605.03450.author: Brielen Madureira et.al.
2605.03450.authors: Brielen Madureira, Mariana Madruga de Brito, Andreas Niekler
